_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec + AK-legislature batch: mark-hunt (WV Auditor R), keith-kautz (WY AG appointed / former Supreme Court Justice), jeremy-bynum (AK HD1 R), rebecca-himschoot (AK HD2 I), andi-story (AK HD3 D). Every TRUE/FALSE cites a URL; null where no evidence; office/candidacy corrections applied (Kautz).

profile.background: 22nd West Virginia State Auditor, sworn in Jan 13 2025 (succeeded JB McCuskey). Won 2024 general 68.7% over Mary Ann Claytor. Long prior career as a DEMOCRAT (WV House of Delegates ~1994-2014, two Democratic congressional primaries incl. 2016 nominee), switched to Republican ~2021-22, WV Senate (SD-8) 2022, then Auditor 2024. Next Auditor election 2028 (no term limits).
profile.confidence_note: Thin issue record: WV does not surface easy roll-call attribution and most of his tenure was as a Democrat, so party is uninformative. Scored only the few documented votes/positions; everything else null. John Birch Society Freedom Index = 42% (2023-24 Senate), a middling score. High headline % reflects a small denominator, not a broad conservative record.

mark-hunt.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED: Mark Hunt is the sitting WV State Auditor (R), not on any 2026 ballot. Documented HARD: cosponsored + voted YEA on SB 10 Campus Self-Defense Act (campus carry, Senate 29-4, Jan 24 2023). Freedom Index credits Yes on vaccine-freedom-in-schools (parental/conscience exemption), raw-milk, 2A protections; No on adopting federal police policy (ambiguous direction - not scored), forced auto insurance, carbon-exchange program. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage, election integrity, immigration. CAVEAT for readers: 25-year Democrat before his recent R registration; weigh the current R label accordingly.

1.note: Freedom Index credits a Yes vote on vaccine-freedom-in-schools (parental/conscience exemption from school vaccine mandates) - supports parental consent on a medical mandate.
Cosponsored and voted YEA on SB 10 Campus Self-Defense Act (campus carry); Freedom Index also credits 2A-protection votes - affirmative pro-gun-rights record opposing firearm restrictions.

profile.background: Wyoming Attorney General, appointed by Gov. Mark Gordon, assumed office July 7 2025. Former Wyoming Supreme Court Justice (2015-2024, retired at mandatory age 70) and District Judge, 8th Judicial District (1993-2015). Succeeded Bridget Hill (whom Gordon elevated to the Supreme Court).
profile.confidence_note: OFFICE CORRECTION: Wyoming's Attorney General is APPOINTED by the Governor (with Senate consent), NOT elected - prior record's 'incumbent seeking reelection' framing was wrong and has been replaced with 'appointed_incumbent'. Most of his record is judicial (rulings = legal reasoning, not policy advocacy) and is left null per scoring discipline. Small denominator; high headline % reflects sparse, not broad, evidence.

keith-kautz.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED + corrected: Keith G. Kautz is the appointed WY Attorney General (since July 7 2025), a former WY Supreme Court Justice - NOT an elected incumbent seeking reelection (WY AG is appointed). HARD on life: after retiring from the bench he publicly joined a Wyoming Family Alliance pray-for-life event (quoted invoking the unborn 'made in your image') and told WPR he asked the Chief Justice not to assign him abortion cases due to his views on 'the right to life and the sanctity of life'; now defends WY's abortion restrictions as AG. He defended his public prayer participation on First Amendment grounds. As AG, WY joined the multistate Google antitrust settlement and Purdue/Sackler opioid settlement (routine multistate joinders - not scored). Judicial dissents (police-negligence liability, juvenile sentencing) treated as legal reasoning, not advocacy. NO EVIDENCE on marriage, education, elections, immigration, guns, federalism.

0.note: Publicly invoked the unborn child 'made in your image' at a pray-for-life event and cited his views on 'the right to life and the sanctity of life' in asking to be recused from abortion cases - affirms personhood from conception.
Publicly professed Christian faith at a prayer event and defended his participation on First Amendment grounds - affirms the right to publicly profess Christ in the public square.

profile.background: Alaska House District 1 (Ketchikan/southern Southeast), sworn Jan 21 2025. First term; former Ketchikan Gateway Borough Assembly, USAF veteran, engineer. Sits in the 19-member House Republican MINORITY caucus; on House Finance. Running for reelection 2026 (votebynum.com).
profile.confidence_note: First-term member: scored from sponsored bills, recorded statements, and reported votes (akleg.gov primary). Genuine conservative on school choice and sound money, with documented pragmatic deviations (voted to override the governor's education-funding veto; authored a smaller PFD; YES on HB 195 flagged by pro-life groups).

jeremy-bynum.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (akleg.gov HD1, R). HARD conservative: prime-sponsored HB 365 Scholarship Granting Organizations (school-choice tax-credit scholarships); cosponsored HB 1 Specie as Legal Tender (gold/silver, sound money/anti-CBDC-adjacent, enacted); cosponsored HB 77 Mail Theft + HB 101 Crimes Against Minors (proportional punishment). Stated 'life begins at conception' (2024 candidate survey). DEVIATION FLAGS: (1) voted YES on HB 195 (2026), which National Right to Life / Alaska Watchman warned could expand pharmacist-dispensed chemical abortion (passed 32-8) - one of 11 Rs in favor; scored FALSE on the abolition cell. (2) reversed to vote YES on the Aug 2025 HB 57 education-funding veto override (raising public-school BSA), breaking with the governor/minority - fiscal-pragmatic, does not map to a rubric penalty cell, noted only. (3) authored a ~$1,500 PFD and voted against the full statutory PFD. NO EVIDENCE on marriage, religious liberty, elections, immigration, federalism. Said he is a 'strong supporter of the 2nd Amendment' (too vague to score a specific cell).

0.note: 2024 candidate survey: stated life 'begins at conception' and supports strengthening adoption.

1.note: Voted YES on HB 195 (2026), a pharmacist-prescribing bill that National Right to Life and Alaska Watchman warned could expand chemical/medication abortion access (passed 32-8) - contrary to the abolition position; one of 11 Republicans in favor.

0.note: Prime sponsor of HB 365 Scholarship Granting Organizations (school-choice / tax-credit scholarships) and HB 333 career/technical training.
Cosponsored HB 1 Specie as Legal Tender (gold/silver as legal tender, enacted Ch.6 SLA 26) - supports decentralized/sound money, anti-CBDC-adjacent.

2.note: Cosponsored HB 77 Mail Theft and HB 101 Crimes Against Minors (raising age of consent to 18) - supports proportional punishment of evildoers.

profile.background: Alaska House District 2 (Sitka/Petersburg/Yakutat); officially Not Affiliated (press: I-Sitka). Retired 30-year elementary teacher; former Sitka Assembly; former State Board of Education appointee. Caucuses with the bipartisan House Majority Coalition; Co-Chair of House Education Committee and the Task Force on Education Funding.
profile.confidence_note: Documented progressive on life, guns, and elections; signature focus is public-school funding (no school-choice support found). 2026 reelection filing not yet confirmed as of research date (term ends Jan 2027).

rebecca-himschoot.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (akleg.gov HD2, Not Affiliated). HARD progressive positions: stated 'I will support policies that allow Alaskans to exercise their constitutional rights, which includes a right to abortion' (2022 KCAW Q&A) and was endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates Alaska; supports keeping ranked-choice voting and open primaries (opposes repeal); supports 'common-sense gun regulations.' Prime sponsor of HB 69, the largest nominal BSA (public-school funding) increase in state history - no school-choice support found. NO EVIDENCE on religious liberty, immigration, CBDC/ESG, bail/crime philosophy, federalism, or a personal marriage/transgender statement (caucus stalled HB 105 but no named Himschoot vote confirmed). 2026 candidacy not yet formally confirmed.

0.note: Stated support for 'a right to abortion' citing Alaska Supreme Court privacy precedent - contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Advocates abortion rights, the opposite of abortion abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates Alaska - accepted an abortion-industry endorsement.

1.note: Stated support for 'advancing common-sense gun regulations' - a gun-control position contrary to opposing new firearm restrictions.

profile.background: Alaska House District 3 (Haines/Skagway/Gustavus/N. Juneau), incumbent since Jan 2019; former Juneau Board of Education (2003-2018). Member of the Alaska House Majority Coalition; Co-Chair of House Education Committee, Vice Chair of House State Affairs. Running for a fifth term in 2026 vs. Republican Annette Kreitzer.
profile.confidence_note: Education-funding-focused Democrat. Few individual issue positions documented beyond a co-sponsorship and education votes; scored only documented-contrary cells, rest null. Small denominator.

andi-story.notes_set: Evidence finalization 2026-06-17. Roster VERIFIED (akleg.gov HD3, D); confirmed running 2026 (fifth term) against Annette Kreitzer (R). HARD: co-sponsor of HB 195 (2026), the pharmacist-prescribing bill pro-life groups (LifeNews/National Right to Life/Alaska Watchman) warned could expand chemical abortion (passed House 32-8) - scored contrary on life. Education record (Co-Chair House Education): voted FOR the HB 57 BSA veto override (May 2025, +$700/student) and the earlier failed +$1,000 override - public-school-funding champion, no school-choice support found. Fiscal language 'opposed overspending from the [Permanent] fund / responsible budgets' (self-described, not scored). Denali-name resolution (situational states-rights framing, not scored). NO EVIDENCE of pro-life, pro-2A, school-choice, voter-ID, pro-police, or marriage/LGBTQ positions. Planned Parenthood endorsement of Story specifically could NOT be confirmed (null).

0.note: Named co-sponsor of HB 195 (2026), which pro-life groups warned could expand chemical/medication abortion access - contrary to affirming protection of life from conception.

1.note: Co-sponsoring a bill to expand abortion-pill access is the opposite of abortion abolition.
